content:
**Brief Answer**  
For **urgent care**, the plan covers visits at **$55 per visit** when done at an in-network urgent care center; if urgent care is received in a hospital outpatient setting, it’s **35% coinsurance** and **subject to the medical deductible**. For the **emergency room**, the ER facility is **35% coinsurance** and **subject to the medical deductible**, while the ER physician is **No Charge**.  

---
**Detailed Explanation**

Because this is a HMO plan, the member must receive services from either an in-network provider or a provider associated with their medical group to be eligible for benefits. A referral from the member's primary care physician (PCP) is usually required when the member wants to see a specialist or other provider. [1]

## A) Urgent Care Services

### 1) Urgent Care – Freestanding outpatient facility services (Urgent care center facility)
**In-Network Provider** [2]  
For in-network providers: [2]  
the plan has a $2,500 deductible per person, not to exceed $5,000 per family. [3]  
However, Urgent Care - Freestanding Outpatient Facility Services is not subject to the deductible. [2]  
The copay for Urgent Care - Freestanding Outpatient Facility Services services is $55. [2]  
The service applies to the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount of $8,750, not to exceed $17,500 per family. When the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount is satisfied, the health plan will reimburse 100% of the allowed amount. [2]  
When seeking urgent care services within their HMO service area, the member needs to verify with their primary care physician (PCP) and Independent Physician Association (IPA) or Medical Group (MG) which urgent care clinics are in-network. If outside the assigned Medical Group Service Area but within California the member may visit any urgent care center. [4]

**Out-of-Network Provider**  
For out-of-network providers, the member is responsible for 100% of the provider's billed amount, as the plan only covers in-network providers.

### 2) Urgent Care – Freestanding professional services (Provider/doctor services at urgent care)
**In-Network Provider** [5]  
For in-network providers: [5]  
the plan has a $2,500 deductible per person, not to exceed $5,000 per family. [3]  
However, Urgent Care - Freestanding Professional Services is not subject to the deductible. [5]  
The copay for Urgent Care - Freestanding Professional Services services is $55. [5]  
The service applies to the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount of $8,750, not to exceed $17,500 per family. When the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount is satisfied, the health plan will reimburse 100% of the allowed amount. [5]  
When seeking urgent care services within their HMO service area, the member needs to verify with their primary care physician (PCP) and Independent Physician Association (IPA) or Medical Group (MG) which urgent care clinics are in-network. If outside the assigned Medical Group Service Area but within California the member may visit any urgent care center. [4]

**Out-of-Network Provider**  
For out-of-network providers, the member is responsible for 100% of the provider's billed amount, as the plan only covers in-network providers.

### 3) Urgent Care – Hospital outpatient facility only
**In-Network Provider** [6]  
For in-network providers: [6]  
the plan has a $2,500 deductible per person, not to exceed $5,000 per family. [3]  
Urgent Care - Outpatient Hospital Facility Only is subject to this deductible. Once the deductible is met, [6]  
The coinsurance is 35% of the allowed amount, and the plan will reimburse the remaining 65% of the allowed amount for Urgent Care - Outpatient Hospital Facility Only. [6]  
The service applies to the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount of $8,750, not to exceed $17,500 per family. When the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount is satisfied, the health plan will reimburse 100% of the allowed amount. [6]  
In addition to the hospital bill, the member can expect related professional fees for services like surgery, anesthesia, and diagnostic testing (radiological, pathology, or laboratory). [7]  
When seeking urgent care services within their HMO service area, the member needs to verify with their primary care physician (PCP) and Independent Physician Association (IPA) or Medical Group (MG) which urgent care clinics are in-network. If outside the assigned Medical Group Service Area but within California the member may visit any urgent care center. [4]

**Out-of-Network Provider**  
For out-of-network providers, the member is responsible for 100% of the provider's billed amount, as the plan only covers in-network providers.

---

## B) Emergency Room (ER) Benefits

Emergency or Ambulance services are covered at the participating provider cost share, even if treatment is received from a non-participating provider.  

A member can access emergency services for an emergency medical condition at any hospital, even if it is a non-participating hospital. If a member is admitted to an out-of-network (OON) or non-participating hospital, the in-network benefits would apply until their condition stabilizes and they're able to be transferred to an in-network hospital. [8]

### 1) Emergency room hospital (ER facility)
**In-Network Provider** [9]  
For in-network providers: [9]  
the plan has a $2,500 deductible per person, not to exceed $5,000 per family. [3]  
Emergency room hospital is subject to this deductible. Once the deductible is met, [9]  
The coinsurance is 35% of the allowed amount, and the plan will reimburse the remaining 65% of the allowed amount for Emergency room hospital. [9]  
The service applies to the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount of $8,750, not to exceed $17,500 per family. When the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount is satisfied, the health plan will reimburse 100% of the allowed amount. [9]

**Out-of-Network Provider**  
For out-of-network providers, the member is responsible for 100% of the provider's billed amount, as the plan only covers in-network providers.

### 2) Emergency room physician (ER professional/physician services)
**In-Network Provider** [10]  
For in-network providers: [10]  
the plan has a $2,500 deductible per person, not to exceed $5,000 per family. [3]  
However, Emergency room physician is not subject to the deductible. [10]  
The plan will reimburse the provider 100% of the allowed amount for Emergency room physician. [10]

**Out-of-Network Provider**  
For out-of-network providers, the member is responsible for 100% of the provider's billed amount, as the plan only covers in-network providers.

### 3) Emergency room services resulting in admission
**In-Network Provider** [11]  
For in-network providers: [11]  
the plan has a $2,500 deductible per person, not to exceed $5,000 per family. [3]  
Emergency room services resulting in admission is subject to this deductible. Once the deductible is met, [11]  
The coinsurance is 35% of the allowed amount, and the plan will reimburse the remaining 65% of the allowed amount for Emergency room services resulting in admission. [11]  
The service applies to the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount of $8,750, not to exceed $17,500 per family. When the plan's out-of-pocket maximum amount is satisfied, the health plan will reimburse 100% of the allowed amount. [11]

**Out-of-Network Provider**  
For out-of-network providers, the member is responsible for 100% of the provider's billed amount, as the plan only covers in-network providers.

---
